channel.title: Idea on Daniels page
channel.description: Recent content in Idea on Daniels page

item.title: Using if else with pipes
item.description: A nice way to use if else within pipes is (see here): library(magrittr) runif(1) %>% magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% {if(.) "foo" else "bar"} ## [1] "bar" Alternatively, the when function from the purrr package can be used: library(purrr, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE) runif(1) %>% purrr::when(. <= 0.5 ~ "foo", . > 0.5 ~ "bar") ## [1] "bar" A nice way to use a vector of inputs is provided by the case_when function from dplyr together with mutate or transmute:

item.title: Using if else with pipes
item.description: A nice way to use if else within pipes is (see here): library(magrittr) runif(1) %>% magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% {if(.) "foo" else "bar"} ## [1] "bar" Alternatively, the when function from the purrr package can be used: library(purrr, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE) runif(1) %>% purrr::when(. <= 0.5 ~ "foo", . > 0.5 ~ "bar") ## [1] "bar" A nice way to use a vector of inputs is provided by the case_when function from dplyr together with mutate or transmute:

item.title: Using if else with pipes
item.description:
A nice way to use if else within pipes is (see here):

library(magrittr)
runif(1) %>% magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% {if(.) "foo" else "bar"}
## [1] "bar"

Alternatively, the when function from the purrr package can be used:

library(purrr, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
runif(1) %>% purrr::when(. <= 0.5 ~ "foo", 
                         . >  0.5 ~ "bar") 
## [1] "bar"

A nice way to use a vector of inputs is provided by the case_when function from dplyr together with mutate or transmute:

library(dplyr,  quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(tibble, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
runif(100) %>% 
  magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% 
  tibble::tibble(a = .) %>% 
  dplyr::transmute(., foobar = case_when(a <= 0.5 ~ "fu", a >  0.5 ~ "bar"))
## # A tibble: 100 x 1
##    foobar
##    <chr> 
##  1 fu    
##  2 fu    
##  3 bar   
##  4 fu    
##  5 fu    
##  6 fu    
##  7 bar   
##  8 bar   
##  9 fu    
## 10 fu    
## # ... with 90 more rows

item.title: Using if else with pipes
item.description:
A nice way to use if else within pipes is (see here):

library(magrittr)
runif(1) %>% magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% {if(.) "foo" else "bar"}
## [1] "bar"

Alternatively, the when function from the purrr package can be used:

library(purrr, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
runif(1) %>% purrr::when(. <= 0.5 ~ "foo", 
                         . >  0.5 ~ "bar") 
## [1] "bar"

A nice way to use a vector of inputs is provided by the case_when function from dplyr together with mutate or transmute:

library(dplyr,  quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
library(tibble, quietly = TRUE, warn.conflicts = FALSE)
runif(100) %>% 
  magrittr::is_greater_than(0.5) %>% 
  tibble::tibble(a = .) %>% 
  dplyr::transmute(., foobar = case_when(a <= 0.5 ~ "fu", a >  0.5 ~ "bar"))
## # A tibble: 100 x 1
##    foobar
##    <chr> 
##  1 fu    
##  2 fu    
##  3 fu    
##  4 fu    
##  5 fu    
##  6 fu    
##  7 bar   
##  8 bar   
##  9 bar   
## 10 bar   
## # ... with 90 more rows

item.title: Feedback with tribbles
item.description: The ordering and display of tables in Rmarkdown (aka data.frames or tibbles) has become so good. I actually prefer it over using print right now. Especially, because I can format in in an intuitive way by using the tribble() function. Here is an example. Let us say I want to summarize the basic set up for an optimization. Some dummy variables might look like this: library(tibble) number_iterations <- 1000 stoping_criterion <- -1 stepsize <- 0.
